title: 10 best herbal tinctures for sore eyes
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for sore eyes are concentrated plant extracts that help alleviate discomfort, redness, and inflammation associated with eye strain or irritation. They are derived from various herbs, such as Calendula officinalis, Euphrasia officinalis, Symphytum officinale, Aloe barbadensis, Echinacea purpurea, Hypericum perforatum, Achillea millefolium, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Aloe vera, and Echinacea angustifolia. If left untreated, sore eyes can lead to vision problems, headaches, and decreased productivity, affecting daily life, work, and overall well-being, making it essential to use these herbal remedies to prevent and alleviate this condition.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with sore eyes because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The aloe vera plant contains a number of beneficial compounds, including vitamins, minerals, and amino acids, that can help to reduce redness and irritation in the eyes. When used as an herbal tincture, these compounds are able to penetrate deeply into the affected area, providing fast and effective relief from sore eyes caused by dryness, fatigue, or minor injuries.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce redness, swelling, and pain in sore eyes.
Antioxidant properties: Aloe vera's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the delicate tissues of the eyes, promoting healing and reducing irritation.

Soothing and protective mucilage: Aloe vera's thick, gel-like substance (mucilage) coats and soothes the surface of the eye, protecting it from further irritation and discomfort while promoting the natural healing process.
remedy_constituents: Glucomannans: These complex carbohydrates help soothe and calm irritated eyes by forming a protective barrier and reducing inflammation.
Aloin: A natural anthraquinone compound, aloin helps reduce eye inflammation and pain by acting as a natural anti-inflammatory and soothing agent.
Vitamins A and E: These antioxidant vitamins help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and promote healing by neutralizing free radicals and supporting eye tissue health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most used part, as they contain the highest concentration of aloe vera's medicinal compounds, including aloin and aloe-emodin, which help soothe and calm sore eyes.
Stems: The stems contain a similar concentration of medicinal compounds as the leaves, making them a popular alternative for sore eye treatments.
Buds: The buds are sometimes used in combination with the leaves or stems to create a tincture that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in sore eyes.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 cup of fresh aloe vera gel from 2-3 mature plants to use for the tincture.
Combine the aloe vera gel with 2 cups of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks to allow the mixture to macerate.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Allow the tincture to mature for an additional 2 weeks before using it as desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower tinctures helps with sore eyes because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated eyes. The herb, also known as Echinacea purpurea, has been used for centuries to treat various eye issues, including conjunctivitis and dry eye syndrome. When applied topically or taken orally, the tincture's antioxidants and flavonoids work together to reduce redness, itchiness, and swelling, providing quick relief from sore eyes and promoting overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Purple Coneflower's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, alleviating discomfort and promoting healing.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The plant's flavonoids and phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sore eyes.

Immune-boosting properties: Purple Coneflower's immunomodulatory compounds stimulate the immune system to fight off infections that may be contributing to eye irritation or inflammation, promoting overall ocular health.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and alleviate soreness in the eyes.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as quercetin, possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation.
Cichoric Acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce inflammation and combat infections that may be contributing to sore eyes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Echinacea purpurea are used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and alkylamides, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Roots: The roots are used for their rich content of polysaccharides and phenolic compounds, which have immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory effects.

Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain a variety of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest a sufficient quantity of fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves with scissors or pinch them off by hand.
Clean the harvested plant material by gently rinsing it with water to remove dirt and debris.
Use a glass jar or container to combine 1 part fresh echinacea purpurea plant material with 2 parts 80 proof vodka or other high-proof spirit.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow the tincture to mature and infuse.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle to separate the liquid from the plant material.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with sore eyes because it contains flavonoids, antioxidants, and carotenoids that work synergistically to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated tissues. The anti-inflammatory properties of pot marigold help to calm redness and itching, while its antioxidant compounds protect the delicate eye tissues from damage caused by free radicals. As a result, pot marigold tinctures provide natural relief for sore eyes, making it an effective remedy for dry eyes, conjunctivitis, and other eye irritations.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as calendic acid and apigenin, help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from itching, burning, and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold tincture, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, protect the eyes from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dry eye syndrome, cataracts, and age-related macular degeneration.

Wound-healing properties: Pot marigold's triterpenoid saponins have been shown to promote wound healing and tissue repair, making it useful for treating conjunctivitis, corneal ulcers, and other eye irritations that can lead to scarring or adhesions.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic Acids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and redness associated with sore eyes.
Carotenoids (specifically, beta-carotene and lutein): These antioxidants protect the eyes from oxidative stress and promote eye health, reducing the severity of sore eyes.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin and kaempferol): These compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate pain, itching, and irritation associated with sore eyes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used to make tinctures for sore eyes due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds.
Leaves: Utilized in tinctures for sore eyes because they contain soothing properties that help reduce inflammation and promote healing.
Roots: Employed in tinctures to address sore eyes, as they contain a range of bioactive compounds that can help calm and protect the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 cup of dried calendula officinalis flowers and 2 cups of 80 proof vodka for tincture.
Combine the dried flowers and vodka in a clean glass jar with a lid tightly.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright tinctures helps with sore eyes because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that effectively soothe and calm irritated eyes. The herb's natural compounds, such as anthraglycosides and flavonoids, work to reduce redness, itchiness, and discomfort associated with dry eye syndrome, blepharitis, and conjunctivitis. By applying a few drops of eyebright tincture to the affected area, it helps to clear mucus and debris, promoting healthy tear flow and restoring visual clarity. As a result, eyebright tinctures provide fast and effective relief for sore eyes, allowing individuals to return to their normal activities with ease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Eyebright tincture contains flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from redness, itching, and irritation.
Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in eyebright tincture help neutralize free radicals that can damage eye tissues and contribute to soreness, fatigue, and dryness.

Anti-infective property: Eyebright's antiseptic properties help combat bacterial, viral, or fungal infections that can cause sore eyes, reducing the risk of complications and promoting faster healing.
remedy_constituents: Phenylethanoid glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in the eyes.
Alkaloids (specifically, rosmarinic acid): As a strong antioxidant, rosmarinic acid helps protect the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation, soothing sore eyes.
Iridoid glycosides (specifically, euphrasine and echinacoside): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which help reduce inflammation and irritation in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Euphrasia officinalis are commonly used to make tinctures for sore eyes because they contain bioactive compounds that help soothe and calm the eye.
Flowers: The flowers are used to make tinctures for sore eyes because they possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that aid in reducing eye inflammation and promoting healing.
Stems: The stems of Euphrasia officinalis are used to make tinctures for sore eyes because they contain bioactive compounds that help to reduce swelling and promote the healing of eye tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh euphrasia officinalis leaves and flowers in the early morning when the dew is still present.
Dry the harvested euphrasia officinalis parts in a warm dark place for 1-2 weeks or until crispy.
Combine 1 part euphrasia officinalis with 2 parts 80% ethanol in a clean glass container to create the tincture.
Steep the mixture for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional gentle shaking to extract the properties.
Strain the euphrasia officinalis tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle to separate the liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with sore eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated tissues. The herb's flavonoids, apigenin, and luteolin, work together to reduce redness and swelling, alleviating discomfort and itchiness. As a natural antihistamine, chamomile also relieves itchy, watery eyes caused by allergies or dryness. Additionally, its gentle, non-irritating nature makes it an excellent choice for sensitive eyes, providing quick and effective relief from soreness and irritation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile tincture helps to reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from redness and discomfort.

Antioxidant: Chamomile's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the delicate tissues of the eyes, reducing the risk of long-term eye damage.
Anxiolytic: The calming effects of chamomile tincture can help to reduce stress and anxiety-related eye strain, which is a common contributor to sore and tired eyes.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid that acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort in sore eyes.
Bisabolol: A sesquiterpene that exhibits anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, providing relief from irritation and redness in sore eyes.
Luteolin: A flavonoid that possesses antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to protect the eyes from damage and promote healing in sore eyes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are commonly used due to their high concentration of chamazulene and apigenin, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help alleviate sore eye symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the eyes, although they may not be as potent as the flowers.
Seeds: The seeds are used due to their potential anti-inflammatory properties, although they are less commonly used than flowers or leaves for sore eye tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers on a dry day when the plant is in full bloom.
Dry the harvested flowers in a dark place for 7 to 10 days at room temperature.
Steep 50g of dried matricilla chamomilla flowers in 500ml of vodka 40% abv for 14 days.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for 4 weeks before using it for medicinal purposes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with sore eyes because of its calming and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal remedy contains linalool, a natural compound that soothes redness and irritation in the eyes. When used topically, it reduces swelling and relieves discomfort associated with dry eyes, conjunctivitis, or other eye irritations. Additionally, lavender's gentle sedative effect can help to relax tense eye muscles, promoting better eye health and reducing the risk of further strain. As a result, English lavender tinctures provide fast and effective relief for sore, itchy, or tired eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness and swelling associated with sore eyes, promoting comfort and relaxation.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant properties of lavender tincture help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and promoting overall eye health.

Calming and sedative properties: Lavender's calming and sedative compounds can help alleviate eye strain, fatigue, and irritability, promoting relaxation and reducing discomfort associated with sore eyes.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Linalool, a terpene present in Lavandula angustifolia, helps to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated eyes, providing relief from soreness and discomfort.

Linalyl acetate: Linalyl acetate, another terpene found in the herb, has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help to calm and relax the eye muscles, alleviating soreness and tension.

Apigenin: Apigenin, a flavonoid phenolic compound present in Lavandula angustifolia, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce swelling, ease pain, and protect the eyes from further damage.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Lavandula angustifolia are used to make tinctures for sore eyes due to their high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Flowers: The flowers are used to make tinctures for sore eyes due to their high concentration of camphor and linalool, which have soothing and anti-inflammatory effects.
Stems: The stems of Lavandula angustifolia are used to make tinctures for sore eyes due to their content of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the flowers completely in a well-ventilated area for 1-2 weeks or until they are completely brittle.
Combine 1 part dried flowers with 2 parts 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar 1-2 times a day.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with sore eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The tannins present in the tree's leaves have been shown to reduce swelling and ease pain, providing fast relief from eye irritations. Additionally, the antioxidants in maidenhair tree help to protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, promoting a healthy and comfortable vision experience. Its soothing properties also aid in reducing redness and itching, making it an effective natural remedy for sore eyes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, alleviating soreness and discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory: The tannins and flavonoids present in Maidenhair Tree tincture have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with sore eyes.
Vasodilatory: Ginkgo biloba's ability to dilate blood vessels can increase blood flow to the eyes, promoting healing, reducing fatigue, and alleviating eye strain.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These antioxidants help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the eyes, alleviating soreness and discomfort.
Bilobalide: As a neuroprotective compound, bilobalide helps protect the optic nerve and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to eye strain and soreness.
Ginkgolides: These diterpene trilactones have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the eyes, while also improving blood flow to promote healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties beneficial for soothing sore eyes.
Seeds: They are used because they contain ginkgolides, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce redness and irritation in the eyes.
Barks: They are used because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids similar to the leaves, which can help soothe and protect the eyes from irritation and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 dried ginkgo biloba leaves from a reputable source in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Chop the leaves into small pieces to release their active compounds and increase their surface area.
Combine the chopped ginkgo biloba leaves with 80-100 proof vodka in a 1:3 ratio by weight for a 1000ml tincture.
Steep the mixture in a dark glass container for 4-6 weeks in a cool dry place, shaking it daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with sore eyes because they contain bioactive compounds that possess anti-inflammatory properties. The tannins and flavonoids present in dandelion extracts have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of conjunctivitis, blepharitis, and dry eye syndrome. By soothing the eyes and reducing redness, dandelion tinctures provide relief from irritation, discomfort, and visual disturbances associated with sore eyes. Additionally, their antioxidant properties help protect the delicate tissues of the eye from further damage, promoting a faster recovery process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help reduce swelling and discomfort in the eyes, alleviating pain and redness.

Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage eye tissues, promoting healthy vision and protecting against age-related macular degeneration and other eye disorders.

Anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties: Dandelion's bioactive compounds have been shown to modulate the immune response, reducing inflammation in the eyes and preventing chronic conditions such as conjunctivitis and blepharitis.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort in the eyes associated with sore eyes.
Kaempferol: This flavonoid phenolic compound has potent antioxidant properties, which help protect the eyes from oxidative damage and reduce inflammation, making it beneficial for sore eyes.
Inulin: This polysaccharide has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help calm the eyes and reduce redness and irritation associated with sore eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make a tincture due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties which can help soothe and protect the eyes.
Flowers: Used to make a tincture due to their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties which can help reduce swelling and infection in the eyes.
Roots: Used to make a tincture due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 1 pint of fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers on a sunny day.
Chop the taraxacum officinale material into small pieces using a sharp knife or scissors.
Combine 1 pint of the chopped taraxacum officinale with 2 cups of 95% ethanol in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the container daily.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lamb's ear tinctures helps with sore eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herb, also known as Stellaria media, has been used for centuries to calm irritated eyes and reduce redness. When taken as a tincture, it absorbs quickly into the bloodstream, providing rapid relief from dryness, itchiness, and burning sensations associated with sore eyes. The tincture's antiseptic properties also help to prevent infection, promoting healing and reducing discomfort. As a result, lamb's ear tinctures have become a natural remedy for soothing and calming sore eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Lamb's ear contains anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid, which help reduce inflammation and redness in the eyes, alleviating symptoms of sore eyes such as swelling and itchiness.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in lamb's ear tincture, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye fatigue and discomfort.

Antibacterial and antiviral activities: Lamb's ear has been shown to exhibit antibacterial and antiviral properties, which can help combat infections that may be causing sore eyes, such as conjunctivitis or pink eye.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds, specifically leonurine and stachysoside, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate soreness and redness in the eyes by reducing inflammation and protecting the delicate tissues from oxidative damage.

Phenolic Acids: The phenolic acids present in Stachys byzantina, such as caffeic acid and ferulic acid, have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe and calm sore eyes by reducing inflammation and combating oxidative stress.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, present in Stachys byzantina, possess strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate soreness and redness in the eyes by reducing inflammation, combating oxidative stress, and promoting the healing of damaged tissues.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are rich in bioactive compounds, making them a popular choice for herbal remedies, including eye soothers.
Flowers: The flowers of Stachys byzantina contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are known for their anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce eye irritation.
Roots: The roots of Stachys byzantina are used for their sedative and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to calm and soothe sore, irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh stachys byzantina leaves in the early morning when the dew is still present on the plants.
Chop 250 grams of the harvested leaves into small pieces using a sharp knife or a herb cutter.
Combine the chopped leaves with 750 milliliters of 80 proof ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena tinctures helps with sore eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's flavonoids and phenolic compounds work to reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with dry or irritated eyes. By soothing the mucous membranes and calming the conjunctiva, lemon verbena tincture provides relief from itchiness and burning sensations, promoting a sense of calm and clarity in the affected area. As a natural remedy, it can be used as an alternative to traditional eye drops, offering a gentle and effective solution for soothing sore eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon verbena tincture contains flavonoids and volatile oils that reduce inflammation and alleviate redness, pain, and swelling associated with sore eyes.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant compounds in lemon verbena tincture help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye fatigue, dryness, and irritation.

Calming and soothing properties: Lemon verbena has a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps to reduce tension and stress that can exacerbate sore eyes, while its soothing properties can help to ease discomfort and promote relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: Iridoids, such as verbascoside, are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and reduce inflammation in sore eyes.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, including verboside, have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help protect the eyes from infections and reduce redness and irritation.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as rutin, have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce swelling and promote healing in sore eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make tinctures for sore eyes due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Used to make tinctures for sore eyes due to their soothing and antimicrobial effects.
Roots: Used to make tinctures for sore eyes due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and flowers on a sunny day in late summer or early fall.
Clean the harvested material thoroughly to remove any dirt or debris from the surface.
Combine 1 part fresh verbena officinalis material with 2 parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and steep for 2-4 weeks shaking daily to extract.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with sore eyes because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated eyes. The herbal extract works to reduce redness, itchiness, and burning sensations caused by dry eyes or allergies. Additionally, valerian's sedative properties can help relax the muscles around the eye, reducing tension and pressure. By alleviating these symptoms, valerian tinctures provide fast and effective relief from sore eyes, allowing for improved vision and reduced discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Valerian root has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, providing relief from dryness, redness, and itchiness.
Antioxidant: Valerian's antioxidant properties can help protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye fatigue and discomfort.
Sedative: Valerian root has a sedative effect that can help calm the nervous system, reducing stress and promoting relaxation, which can alleviate eye strain and tension.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are rich in essential oils and sesquiterpenes, which make them an effective remedy for soothing inflammation and pain associated with sore eyes.
Buds: Valerian buds contain a high concentration of flavonoids and alkaloids that help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, making them a popular choice for treating irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried valeriana officinalis roots and cut them into small pieces suitable for extraction.
Combine the root pieces with 60% vodka in a glass jar at a ratio of 1 part herb to 5 parts solvent.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks to allow for maceration and extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass jar discarding the solids and preserving the liquid.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place for up to 6 months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tinctures helps with sore eyes because of its unique ability to soothe and calm irritated tissues. The herb's natural anti-inflammatory properties work to reduce redness and swelling, while its antioxidant-rich compounds help to protect the delicate skin around the eyes from further damage. As a result, comfrey tincture can provide fast and effective relief for dry, itchy, or burning eyes, making it an excellent remedy for individuals experiencing discomfort or irritation due to allergies, exhaustion, or other environmental factors.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Comfrey tincture helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, soothing discomfort and redness associated with eye strain or irritation.
Antioxidant properties: Comfrey's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can damage eye tissues and contribute to age-related macular degeneration, promoting overall eye health.

Soothing and protective mucilage properties: Comfrey tincture's mucilages (gum-like substances) form a protective film on the surface of the eyes, shielding them from further irritation and soothing dryness or itchiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of comfrey are rich in allantoin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Buds: Comfrey buds contain rosmarinic acid, an antioxidant that reduces inflammation and promotes healing, making them effective for treating sore eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh symphytum officinale leaves and stems in the morning when they are at their highest concentration of medicinal compounds.
Cut the plant material into small pieces and combine it with a neutral-tasting liquid such as vodka or ethanol at a ratio of 1 part herb to 3 parts solvent.
Place the mixture in a clean glass jar and seal it tightly, allowing it to steep for 14 days in a cool dark place.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container, squeezing the solids gently to extract as much liquid as possible.
Transfer the tincture to a dropper bottle and store it in the refrigerator at a temperature of 40°f (4°c) for up to one year, shaking well before each use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tinctures helps with sore eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated tissues. The plantain's natural oils and compounds work to reduce swelling and ease discomfort, providing relief from redness and itchiness. As a natural antiseptic, plantain tincture also helps to cleanse the eye area, removing impurities and debris that can exacerbate sore eyes. Additionally, its gentle, non-irritating properties make it an excellent treatment option for sensitive eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Plantain tincture reduces inflammation and swelling in the eye, providing relief from redness and discomfort. This is due to its ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.

Antioxidant property: Plantain tincture's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dryness, itchiness, and fatigue.

Soother property: Plantain tincture has a calming effect on irritated eye tissues, providing rapid relief from burning, itching, and stinging sensations, thanks to its ability to reduce histamine release and promote relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Plantain roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with sore eyes.
Barks: The barks of the plantain plant contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which can help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried plantago major leaves measure one cup.
Chop the harvested plantago major leaves into small pieces using scissors.
Combine chopped plantago major leaves with 80 proof vodka measuring two cups.
Allow the mixture to steep for four weeks in a cool dark place shaking daily.
Strain the liquid and discard solids filtering through cheesecloth and store.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose tinctures helps with sore eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal extract contains salicylic acid, a natural derivative of aspirin that effectively soothes and calms irritated ocular tissues. When used as a topical solution or added to eye drops, damask rose tinctures can provide rapid relief from sore eyes, reducing redness, itchiness, and dryness for a more comfortable visual experience.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content of damask rose tincture helps protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye strain and discomfort.
Soften and moisturize: Damask rose tincture's emollient properties help soften and moisturize the tear ducts and conjunctiva, reducing dryness, irritation, and itchiness in sore eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to make tinctures for sore eyes because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce redness and swelling.
Flowers: Damask rose flowers are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing the appearance of puffiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest rosa damascena flowers at peak bloom in the morning when dew is still present.
Clean and dry the harvested flowers by gently shaking off excess moisture and spread out on paper towels.
Combine 100 grams of dried rosa damascena flowers with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and labeling with date of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with sore eyes because they contain antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties that soothe irritated eyes. The tannins in elderberry help to reduce inflammation and redness, while the antioxidants work to combat oxidative stress and protect the delicate tissues of the eyes. Additionally, elder tincture's antimicrobial properties can help to combat infections that may be contributing to sore eyes. Overall, elder tinctures provide a natural and effective way to alleviate sore eyes and promote healthy vision.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: Elder tincture contains antioxidants like vitamin C and anthocyanins, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, soothing sore and irritated eyes.

Anti-inflammatory: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in elder tincture have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with sore eyes, such as conjunctivitis or blepharitis.

Immune system support: Elder tincture is known for its immunomodulatory effects, which can help boost the body's natural defense against eye infections and inflammation, promoting a faster recovery from sore eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots have been used for centuries to treat eye problems, including conjunctivitis and inflammation, due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.

Buds: The buds of the elder plant contain high levels of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective in soothing sore eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra flowers at dawn when they are in full bloom for maximum potency.
Chop the flowers into small pieces and mix them with vodka in a clean glass jar one part flowers to five parts vodka.
Allow the mixture to macerate for six weeks in a cool dark place shaking the jar daily to ensure thorough extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids and retaining the tincture.
Store the final tincture in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid at room temperature for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with sore eyes because of its calming and soothing properties, which can effectively reduce inflammation and relieve discomfort. Sedative and Antispasmodic effects: Lemon balm's sedative and antispasmodic properties can help calm and relax the muscles surrounding the eyes, reducing eye strain, spasms, and twitching associated with sore eyes.

remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing eye inflammation and discomfort.
Leaves: The leaves of the lemon balm plant contain high amounts of citral, a natural anti-inflammatory that helps soothe and calm irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves in the morning after dew has dried for optimal potency retention.
Rinse the harvested leaves with filtered water and pat dry with paper towels to remove excess moisture.
Combine 250 grams of dried melissa officinalis leaves with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain and filter the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean dark glass bottle and discard solids after two hours of standing time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with sore eyes because it possesses anti-inflammatory properties that effectively reduce redness and swelling. remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with sore eyes.
Seeds: Containing natural oils that soothe and calm irritated eye tissue, providing relief from dry, itchy, or inflamed eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves in the morning after dew has dried.
Rinse the harvested leaves thoroughly with filtered water and pat dry gently.
Combine one part of the dried leaves with 10 parts of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture at room temperature for six weeks, shaking the jar daily for eight minutes.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle and discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tinctures helps with sore eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive compounds in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with dry or irritated eyes. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves or flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Rinse the plant material with distilled water and gently pat it dry with clean paper towels.
Combine 200g of the dried thymus vulgaris material with 500ml of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 14 days in a cool dark place shaking the jar twice daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean dark glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with sore eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers, ensuring 100g of plant material.
Cleanse the plant material by gently washing with distilled water to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for solvent extraction.
Combine chopped plant material with 500ml of 95% ethanol in a glass jar, allowing maceration for 2 weeks.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, transferring it to dark glass bottles for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram tinctures helps with sore eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried oregano leaves from a trusted source and measure out 20% of the total weight in grams.
Combine the measured oregano leaves with 80% vodka by weight in a clean glass jar ensuring thorough coverage.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking occasionally to facilitate extraction.
After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids.
Transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles, label and date them, and store in a cool dark place for up to 5 years.
